title: Gate pane semantic summary
goal: Make the Herdr Integration Gate / Repair pane answer gate-specific state questions instead of falling back to generic monitor/feed text when idle.
acceptance: When a gate or repair command is active, the Gate / Repair pane shows compact live command identity, elapsed time, bounded tail output, and pass/fail/running state from daemon-owned gate/shell progress data.

When idle, the Gate / Repair pane shows a gate-scoped summary rather than the generic monitor feed: verification profile, latest gate result, last gate failure if any, repair policy/state, handoff readiness if known, and next gate/repair-relevant commands.
The gate pane consumes FEED-02 projection fields or daemon-owned gate/shell artifacts; it never reads Herdr scrollback, pane text, or UI state as workflow evidence.

Golden fixtures cover active command rendering, idle passed state, idle failed/repairable state, no-gate-yet state, and absence of generic monitor sections such as full run activity or unrelated worker feed entries.
Gate pane rendering is display-only: command exit status, stdout/stderr artifacts, typed gate results, repair decisions, verification, merge, and handoff readiness remain daemon-owned and unchanged.
Renderer failures remain non-fatal visibility incidents or pane warnings and do not change gate result, repair policy, run status, slice status, merge, final SHA, or handoff behavior.
Normal cargo test remains portable without Herdr; real Herdr/cockpit behavior stays behind explicit Herdr-gated tests or manual evidence.
must_ask_if: The implementation would make Herdr required for gate or repair execution, verification, merge, or handoff.
The implementation would parse terminal scrollback or pane text as gate/repair correctness evidence.
The implementation would alter gate result, repair decisions, verification, merge, or handoff based on painter output.
The implementation would reintroduce generic monitor/feed fallback as the normal idle Gate / Repair pane instead of a gate-scoped summary.
The implementation would remove or weaken daemon-owned gate/shell result artifacts.
The implementation would duplicate FEED-02 projection wording instead of reusing the shared projection/gate-summary seam.
